summary: Verified bug fix: auto-calculation of Total field in sale edit modal. All 9 acceptance criteria passed: modal opens with current values, auto-calc triggers on qty/price change, manual override is respected, label and help text present, bg-slate-50 styling present, backend persists correctly and metrics update after save.
passed: Backend login OK (admin@ventas.com / admin123) returns valid JWT
Edit modal opens with original buggy sale data (qty=6, price=10000, total=10000)
Label '(auto-calculado: cantidad × precio)' is visible next to Total
Help text 'Puedes modificar manualmente para aplicar descuentos' visible below Total
Total input has bg-slate-50 class (gray background)
Changing qty=2 (with price=10000) auto-updated total to 20000
Changing price=5000 (with qty=2) auto-updated total to 10000
Manual Total override (8000) is respected after auto-calc
Setting qty=6 + price=10000 produced Total=60000 (the core bug-fix scenario)
Save persisted to backend: sale now shows '6 x $10.000' = $60.000 with toast 'Registro actualizado'
Tienda B Ganancia recalculated after save (was negative buggy value, now updated based on correct totals)
minor_notes: Filling qty with same value (6→6) does not trigger onChange so total stays unchanged — expected React behavior, not a bug.
During testing 2 extra sales appeared in sidebar likely from accidental Enter key on login form pre-fill earlier; does not affect bug-fix verification.
